name: english-speaking-training-vscode
description: Standalone VS Code speaking-practice cockpit for local EnglishSpeakingTraining files.

englishTraining.pythonPath.description: Python executable used only for optional local progress completion.

englishTraining.timezone.description: Timezone used for daily package dates.

englishTraining.localMaterialsRoot.description: Optional absolute path to a local training materials root that contains prebuilt/. When set, the extension can read local materials from any VS Code workspace.

englishTraining.coachProvider.description: Language-model provider used for transcript correction and follow-up generation. Default routes through OpenAI (gpt-4o) so the full OpenAI stack only needs an OpenAI key; Gemini and Xiaomi MiMo remain available as alternates.

englishTraining.audioUnderstandingProvid.description: Speech input provider. OpenAI is the default and routes through /v1/audio/transcriptions (gpt-4o-transcribe) for accurate file-based transcription, or Realtime WebSocket if openaiTranscriptionMode is set to realtime. Gemini and Xiaomi MiMo remain available as alternates.

englishTraining.ttsProvider.description: Speech output provider used to generate the native-version follow-along audio. Default uses OpenAI gpt-4o-mini-tts with instructions-based style control (marin voice); Gemini, Qwen-TTS, and Xiaomi MiMo remain available as fallbacks.

englishTraining.openaiRealtimeTranscript.description: OpenAI Realtime transcription model used when openaiTranscriptionMode is set to realtime. gpt-realtime-whisper is the official choice for live transcript deltas.

englishTraining.openaiTranscriptionMode.description: OpenAI transcription transport. 'file' uses /v1/audio/transcriptions with openaiFileTranscriptionModel and supports a domain prompt for higher accuracy (recommended for bounded recordings). 'realtime' uses /v1/realtime WebSocket for low-latency transcript deltas.

englishTraining.openaiFileTranscriptionM.description: Model used when openaiTranscriptionMode is 'file'. gpt-4o-transcribe is the latest quality/latency default; gpt-4o-mini-transcribe is faster; gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize adds speaker labels; whisper-1 supports verbose timestamps and SRT/VTT.

englishTraining.openaiCoachModel.description: OpenAI chat model used for transcript correction and follow-up coaching when the coach provider is set to OpenAI. Calls the chat-completions JSON endpoint.

englishTraining.openaiTtsModel.description: OpenAI model used for native-version follow-along audio. gpt-4o-mini-tts supports the 'instructions' field for accent, emotion, intonation, and style control. tts-1 and tts-1-hd remain available but do not accept instructions.

englishTraining.openaiTtsVoice.description: OpenAI voice used for native-version follow-along audio. The enum lists OpenAI's built-in speech voices: alloy, ash, ballad, coral, echo, fable, nova, onyx, sage, shimmer, verse, marin, and cedar. The default is marin.

englishTraining.openaiTtsInstructions.description: Optional instructions passed to gpt-4o-mini-tts to steer accent, emotional range, intonation, speed, tone, or whispering. When blank, the coach is asked to emit a tts_style suggestion per turn; if that is also blank, a clear-and-patient academic default is used.

englishTraining.openaiTtsResponseFormat.description: OpenAI TTS audio container. wav and pcm are recommended for the lowest first-audio latency because they require no decoding; mp3 is the smallest file. pcm is raw 24kHz signed 16-bit little-endian samples without a header.

englishTraining.geminiCoachModel.description: Gemini model used for correction and follow-up coaching. Gemini 3 Flash is the default; Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview and 3.1 Flash-Lite variants are available from Google AI Studio for stronger reasoning or lower latency.

englishTraining.geminiAudioUnderstanding.description: Gemini model used for speech input understanding/transcription when speech in is set to Gemini. Google audio-understanding examples use gemini-3-flash-preview; 3.1 Pro and Flash-Lite options are available when you want quality or speed tradeoffs.

englishTraining.geminiTtsModel.description: Gemini model used for native-version follow-along audio. Uses the latest Google AI Studio TTS preview through generativelanguage.googleapis.com generateContent with responseModalities=AUDIO.

englishTraining.geminiTtsVoice.description: Gemini prebuilt voice name for native-version audio.

englishTraining.mimoAnthropicBaseUrl.description: Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan Anthropic-compatible base URL. Used for mimo-v2.5 coach via /v1/messages. Distinct vendor and key from DashScope/Qwen-TTS.

englishTraining.mimoCoachModel.description: Xiaomi MiMo model used for correction and follow-up coaching via Anthropic-compatible endpoint.

englishTraining.mimoAudioBaseUrl.description: Xiaomi MiMo OpenAI-compatible base URL used for speech-input (audio understanding) when speech in is set to MiMo. The endpoint posts to <base>/chat/completions.

englishTraining.mimoAudioUnderstandingMo.description: Xiaomi MiMo multimodal model used to transcribe spoken English when speech in is set to MiMo.

englishTraining.mimoTtsBaseUrl.description: Xiaomi MiMo OpenAI-compatible base URL used for speech-output (TTS) when speech out is set to MiMo. The endpoint posts to <base>/chat/completions.

englishTraining.mimoTtsModel.description: Xiaomi MiMo TTS model used for native-version follow-along audio when speech out is set to MiMo.

englishTraining.mimoTtsVoice.description: Xiaomi MiMo built-in voice for native-version audio. Mia and Chloe are female; Milo and Dean are male.

englishTraining.qwenTtsEndpoint.description: DashScope Qwen-TTS multimodal generation endpoint. Use the intl endpoint only with a matching international DashScope API key.

englishTraining.qwenTtsModel.description: Qwen-TTS model used for native-version audio. qwen3-tts-flash is the default; qwen3-tts-instruct-flash accepts optional speaking instructions.

englishTraining.qwenTtsVoice.description: Qwen-TTS voice used for native-version follow-along audio.

englishTraining.qwenTtsLanguageType.description: Qwen-TTS language_type sent with speech-output requests. Use Auto for mixed or uncertain language; a specific language usually improves single-language synthesis quality.

englishTraining.qwenTtsInstructions.description: Optional Qwen-TTS speaking instructions. Sent only when qwen3-tts-instruct-flash is selected.

englishTraining.ttsSpeed.description: Playback speed used when generating follow-along TTS audio.

englishTraining.recordSampleRate.description: Sample rate in Hz written by the native macOS recorder. Default 48000 matches built-in iMac/MacBook microphones and avoids AVFoundation's lossy internal sample-rate conversion, which is the main cause of muffled / noisy takes when forced to 16000. Downstream STT resamples to whatever the model needs (16 kHz for Whisper-family, internal for gpt-4o-transcribe), so a higher capture rate is pure quality upside.

englishTraining.recorderBackend.description: Recording backend. macLocal uses macOS AVFoundation through ffmpeg and avoids iPhone/Continuity microphones; webview uses VS Code MediaRecorder; auto tries webview and falls back to native.

englishTraining.preferredMicrophoneName.description: Optional microphone name substring to prefer for recording, for example iMac Microphone. Used by both webview selection and native macOS recording.

englishTraining.blockedMicrophoneNamePat.description: Case-insensitive regex for microphone names the recorder should avoid, especially iPhone/Continuity devices.

englishTraining.nativeRecorderFfmpegPath.description: ffmpeg executable used for macOS local recording.

englishTraining.nativeRecorderFfmpegAudi.description: macOS AVFoundation audio device index for ffmpeg recording, or auto to choose a local Mac microphone while avoiding blocked devices. Use ffmpeg -f avfoundation -list_devices true -i "" to inspect devices.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run typecheck && npm run bundle
